questions: The normal haemoglobin level in adult males is *13-17* g/dL and in adult females is *12-15* g/dL.
Erythropoiesis is stimulated by *erythropoietin*, a hormone produced mainly by the *kidneys* in response to hypoxia.
The intrinsic pathway of coagulation is activated by contact with *collagen/subendothelium* and is measured by the *APTT/aPTT* test.
ABO blood group antigens are present on the *red blood cell* surface, and the corresponding *antibodies* are present in the plasma.
Iron is absorbed mainly in the *duodenum* and transported in blood bound to *transferrin*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
